title: Audit Log Retention Compliance
spl:
index=_internal source=*metrics.log* group=per_index_thruput series=healthcare_epic
| timechart span=1d sum(kb) as volume
| appendcols [ search index=_audit action=delete NOT user=system | timechart span=1d count as deletes ]
description: Verifies clinical and security logs meet retention policy and are not silently dropped.
value: Verifies clinical and security logs meet retention policy and are not silently dropped.
implementation: Legal hold overrides; document cold storage for long retention; alert on forwarder silence > 1h for PHI indexes.
visualization: Line chart (ingest volume), Table (delete operations), Single value (days of coverage).
knownFalsePositives: Retention checks can flag delayed archive jobs during index migrations or legal holds; treat as ops hygiene before compliance escalation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk internal, `_audit` index.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_internal`, `index=_audit`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Legal hold overrides; document cold storage for long retention; alert on forwarder silence > 1h for PHI indexes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal source=*metrics.log* group=per_index_thruput series=healthcare_epic
| timechart span=1d sum(kb) as volume
| appendcols [ search index=_audit action=delete NOT user=system | timechart span=1d count as deletes ]
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit Log Retention Compliance** — Verifies clinical and security logs meet retention policy and are not silently dropped.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_internal`, `index=_audit`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk internal, `_audit` index. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_internal. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Adds columns from a subsearch with `appendcols`.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (ingest volume), Table (delete operations), Single value (days of coverage).
grandmaExplanation: In Healthcare, we pay close attention to "Audit Log Retention Compliance" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
